zh: Carol 和 Tom 邀请我们共进午餐。透过窗户看看。
en: Carol and Tom invited us to lunch. Look through the window.
It's nice and warm out here.
Come and have something to drink.

zh: 请给我一杯啤酒好吗？ CAROL：啤酒？
en: May I have a glass of beer please? CAROL: Beer?

zh: 她最后一句的‘改变’是什么意思？
en: What does she mean by'change'in the last sentence?

zh: 今天早上走进餐厅时，他把一些硬币掉在了地板上。
en: into the dining room this morning, he dropped some coins on the floor.

zh: 到处都是硬币。我们寻找他们，
en: There were coins everywhere. We looked for them,
but we could not find them all.

zh: 当我们吃早餐时，我们的小男孩， Tommy，
en: While we were having breakfast, our little boy, Tommy,

zh: 在地板上发现了两枚小硬币。
en: found two small coins on the floor.

zh: 他把它们都放进嘴里。我们都试图拿到硬币，
en: He put them both into his mouth. We both tried to get the coins,

zh: Tommy 已经把它们吞掉了！
当我做家务时，我丈夫给我打电话
en: when I was doing the housework, my husband phoned me

zh: Tommy 怎么样？”他问道。“我不知道，”我回答道，“Tommy 今天早上去了厕所三次，
en: How's Tommy?'he asked. 'I don't know,'I answered, 'Tommy's been to the toilet three times this morning,
but I haven't had any change yet!'

zh: 是谁在黑暗中向盗贼喊话？
en: Who called out to the thieves in the dark?

zh: 我想告诉你一个真实的故事。
en: I want to tell you a true story.

zh: 一年前，这件事发生在我的一个朋友身上。
en: It happened to a friend of mine a year ago.

zh: 当我的朋友 George 正在床上看书时，两个小偷爬进了他的厨房。
en: While my friend, George, was reading in bed, two thieves climbed into his kitchen.

zh: 进了屋子之后，他们就进了餐厅。天很黑，
en: After they had entered the house, they went into the dining room. It was very dark,

zh: 于是他们打开了手电筒。
en: so they turned on a torch.

zh: 突然，他们听到背后有声音。 “怎么了？怎么了？”有人喊道。窃贼丢下了火把
en: Suddenly, they heard a voice behind them. 'What's up?What's up?'someone called. The thieves dropped the torch
and ran away as quickly as they could.

zh: 但 George 的鹦鹉 Henry 还在那里。“怎么了， George？”他喊道。
en: But George's parrot, Henry, was still there. 'What's up, George?'he called.

zh: “没什么， Henry，”George 微笑着说道。“回去睡觉吧。”
en: 'Nothing, Henry,'George said and smiled. 'Go back to sleep.'

zh: 为什么 Caroline 没有立即认出这位顾客？
en: Why didn't Caroline recognize the customer straight away?

zh: 半小时前这里有两本昂贵的词典，
en: two expensive dictionaries here half an hour ago,

zh: 站在柜台后面的那位女士。
en: The lady who is standing behind the counter.

zh: Caroline，半小时前您为这位先生服务过吗？他说他就是那个男人
en: Did you serve this gentleman half an hour ago, Caroline? He says he's the man
Have you got a hat, sir?
Would you put it on, please?

zh: 你服务过的， Caroline？ CAROLINE：是的。
en: that you served, Caroline? CAROLINE: Yes.

zh: 那个留胡子的男人是谁？
en: Who is the man with the beard?

zh: 这是我去澳大利亚旅行时拍的照片。
en: This is a photograph I took during my trip to Australia.

zh: 他们是我在旅途中遇到的人。
en: They're people I met during the trip.

zh: 这就是我跟你说过的那个人。
en: That's the man I told you about.

zh: 在澳大利亚找到一份工作。

en: I grew a beard during the trip,
Why did you shave it off?

zh: 现在喝茶吧， Peter？ PETER：还没有。
en: and have tea now, Peter? PETER: Not yet.
I must water the garden first.
Do you have to water it now?
Last summer it was very dry, too.
I had to water it every day.
Well, I'll have tea by myself.

zh: 你已经完成了吗？ PETER：是的。
en: Have you finished already? PETER: Yes.
you don't need to water the garden.

zh: 这意味着我可以喝茶了。
en: It means I can have tea, instead.
is only twenty nine, and why is it unclear?

zh: 你能认出那个女人吗， Liz？
en: Can you recognize that woman, Liz?

zh: 一定是 Karen Marsh，女演员。
en: It must be Karen Marsh, the actress.

zh: Isn 他不是她的第三任丈夫吗？
不，他一定是她的第四个或第五个。
en: No.He must be her fourth or fifth.

zh: Doesn 不是 Karen Marsh 看起来老了！
但她至少得有四十岁了。
en: but she must be at least forty.
when I was still at school.

zh: 那是很久以前的事了，不是吗？
en: That was a long time ago, wasn't it?

zh: 我自己还不到二十九岁。
en: I'm not more than twenty-nine myself.

zh: 那个警察正在向你招手。他要你停下来。
en: That policeman's waving to you. He wants you to stop.
Where do you think you are?

zh: 你一定是以每小时七十英里的速度开车的。
en: You must have been driving at seventy miles an hour.

zh: Didn 你没看到速度限制吗？ GARY：恐怕我没有，警官。
en: Didn't you see the speed limit? GARY: I’m afraid I didn't, officer.
I was telling him to drive slowly.

zh: 这就是为什么我没有看到这个标志。
en: That's why I didn't see the sign.

zh: 让我看看你的驾驶执照。
en: Let me see your driving licence.
I won't charge you this time.

zh: 但你最好不要再这样做了！ GARY：谢谢。
en: But you'd better not do it again! GARY: Thank you.

zh: 我告诉过你开慢点， Gary。
en: I told you to drive slowly, Gary.
What's the problem about deciding on a holiday?

zh: 今年度假， Gary？ GARY : 我们可能会出国。
en: spend your holidays this year, Gary? GARY: We may go abroad.
My wife wants to go to Egypt.
I'd like to go there, too.
We can't make up our minds.

zh: 您会乘船还是乘飞机旅行？
en: Will you travel by sea or by air?
but it takes a long time.

zh: 我相信你会玩得很开心。
我的妻子总是担心太多。谁来照顾狗？
en: My wife always worries too much. Who's going to look after the dog?
Who's going to look after the house?

zh: 谁来照看花园？我们每年都会遇到这个问题。
en: Who's going to look after the garden? We have this problem every year.
In the end, we stay at home

zh: 一部新电影， Marsh 小姐？ MISS MARSH：是的，我有。
en: a new film, Miss Marsh? MISS MARSH: Yes, I have.
Are you going to make another?

zh: 我很长一段时间都不想再拍一部电影了。
en: I don't want to make another film for a long time.

zh: 我们去买份报纸吧， Liz。
' Karen Marsh： Sensational 新闻！由我们的记者 Alan Jones 报道。
en: 'Karen Marsh: Sensational News! By our reporter, Alan Jones.

zh: 她穿着蓝色连衣裙和貂皮大衣。
en: She was wearing a blue dress and a mink coat.

zh: 她刚刚拍了一部新电影。她说
en: she had just made a new film. She said

zh: 她不会再做第二次。她说她要退休了。
en: she was not going to make another. She said she was going to retire.

zh: 她告诉记者，她感觉很累，不想做。
en: She told reporters she felt very tired and didn't want to make

zh: 另一部电影很长一段时间了。我想知道为什么！
en: another film for a long time.' I wonder why!

zh: 您认为 Karen Marsh 会退休吗？
en: Is Karen Marsh going to retire, do you think?

zh: Marsh 小姐，要退休了吗？ MISS MARSH：我可以。
en: going to retire, Miss Marsh? MISS MARSH: I may.
I can't make up my mind.

zh: 他不会让我再拍一部电影。
en: He won't let me make another film.

zh: 你未来的丈夫， Marsh 小姐？
是的，让我把他介绍给你。
en: Yes.Let me introduce him to you.
We're going to get married next week.
She said she would have to

zh: 然后她向我们介绍了 Carlos 并告诉我们
en: Then she introduced us to Carlos and told us
they would get married next week.'

zh: 这是一个轰动的消息，不是吗？
他将成为她的第六任丈夫！
如果 Julie 有钱，她想做什么？
en: What would Julie like to do, if she had the money?
Are you doing the football pools, Brian?

zh: 我确信这周我们会赢得一些东西。
en: I’m sure we'll win something this week.

zh: 但我们从来没有赢得任何东西！你会怎么做
en: but we never win anything! What will you do

zh: 如果你赢了很多钱？ BRIAN :如果我赢了很多钱
en: if you win a lot of money? BRIAN: If I win a lot of money

zh: 我给你买一件貂皮大衣。
en: I'll buy you a mink coat.
I don't want a mink coat!
I want to see the world.
If we win a lot of money

zh: 我们会住在最好的酒店。
en: and we'll stay at the best hotels.

zh: 并在乡下买一栋大房子。
en: and buy a big house in the country.

zh: 我们将会有一个美丽的花园......
en: We'll have a beautiful garden and...

zh: 但如果我们花掉所有的钱
en: But if we spend allthat money

zh: 如果我们花掉所有的钱
en: If we spend all the money

zh: 我们会努力再次赢得足球赛冠军。
en: we'lltry and win the football pools again.

zh: 告诉 Mary 我们会迟到
Hasn Mary 没有告诉过你吗？
她今晚邀请 Charlotte 和我共进晚餐。
en: She invited Charlotte and me to dinner this evening.
at your house at six o'clock,
I'll have to stay at the office.

zh: 我不知道什么时候能完成。
en: I don't know when I'll finish.

zh: 如果 Mary 需要任何帮助。

en: I don't know what you're talking about.
That is John Smith, isn't it?

zh: 你是 John Smith，工程师，不是吗？
en: You are John Smith, the engineer, aren't you?

zh: 对于 Overseas Engineering 公司，不是吗？
en: for the Overseas Engineering Company, don't you?

zh: 我是 John Smith 电话工程师
en: I'm John Smith the telephone engineer

zh: 我正在修理你的电话线。
en: and I'm repairing your telephone line.

zh: 上周，我四岁的女儿 Sally 受邀参加一个儿童聚会。
en: Last week, my four-year-old daughter, Sally, was invited to a children's party.
I decided to take her by train.

zh: 因为她以前从未坐过火车。
en: because she had never travelled on a train before.

zh: 突然，一位中年女士上了火车
en: Suddenly, a middle-aged lady got on the train

zh: 这位女士穿着一件蓝色外套，戴着一顶滑稽的大帽子。
en: The lady was dressed in a blue coat and a large, funny hat.
After the train had left the station,

zh: 这位女士打开手提包，拿出粉饼。然后她开始化妆。
en: the lady opened her handbag and took out her powder compact. She then began to make up her face.

zh: “你为什么要这么做？” Sally 问道。
“为了让自己变得美丽，”女士回答道。她收起粉盒，露出友善的微笑。
en: 'To make myself beautiful,'the lady answered. She put away her compact and smiled kindly.

zh: 标牌上的字有什么好笑的？
en: What was so funny about the words on the sign?

zh: 我住在一个非常古老的城镇
en: I live in a very old town

zh: 周围环绕着美丽的树林。是著名的风景区。
en: which is surrounded by beautiful woods. It is a famous beauty spot.

zh: 从城里来看看我们的小镇
en: come from the city to see our town

zh: 并步行穿过树林。 Visitors 已被询问
en: and to walk through the woods. Visitors have been asked
to keep the woods clean and tidy.

zh: Litter 篮子已经放在树下，但人们仍然把垃圾扔得到处都是。上周三，我去树林里散步。我看到了什么
en: Litter baskets have been placed under the trees, but people still throw their rubbish everywhere. Last Wednesday, I went for a walk in the woods. What I saw
and the ground was covered with

zh: 纸片、烟头、旧轮胎、空瓶子和生锈的罐头。
en: pieces of paper, cigarette ends, old tyres, empty bottles and rusty tins.

zh: 我发现了一个牌子，上面写着：
en: I found a sign which said,

zh: “任何在树林里乱扔垃圾的人都会受到起诉！”
en: 'Anyone who leaves litter in these woods will be prosecuted!'
